calendar.matters:
Amended HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-22-2024 Off Calendar

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. GARY BEMIS, Esq. of Legal Department of GCFS, Inc. (tel: 800-646-4237) for Plaintiff GCFS, Inc.
Defendant HAROLD SMITH, in pro per present (tel: 415-678-7924).

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF GCFS INC's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

***** After the hearing, the Court signs an order granting in part the claim of exemption (see signed order for complete ruling.)(302/RBU)*****

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-01-2024 Continued To May-22-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Defendant HAROLD SMITH, in pro per present (tel: 415-678-7924).
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF GCFS INC.'s HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Continued to May 22, 2024. Defendant apparently claims that the levied funds in his US Bank account are exempt Social Security payments. (See Kilker v. Stillman (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 320; CCP 704.080.) Defendant, however, does not trace those funds to an exempt source. (CCP 703.080.) Defendant has leave to provide his bank statements or other evidence to the court and plaintiff by May 13, 2024, to demonstrate that the funds are exempt.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jun-09-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harold D. Smith, 415-678-7924 for Defendant in Pro Per (In Person)
No appearance is made by Plaintiff GCFS, Inc.

Hearing is held. NOT ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GCFS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The claim of exemption is granted. The levying officer is directed to release all of debtor Smith's funds that it is currently holding back to the debtor. Debtor sufficiently establishes that they are exempt funds. (See CCP secs. 704.130, 704.160, 703.080.) The court will execute a judicial council form of order reflecting this tentative ruling.

Order ?

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-23-2022 Continued To Jun-09-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GCFS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Continued to June 9, 2022. Defendant shall present his bank records to demonstrate that all of the money that the sheriff is currently holding is the result of exempt payments. If possible, please file with the court or email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org and serve on plaintiff's counsel, a copy of those records by June 3, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Sep-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harold Smith, In Pro Per (415-678-7924) appears as self-represented defendant/judgment debtor/exemption claimant

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CLAIM OF EXEMPTION of Harold Smith is GRANTED, except that the Court issues an order requiring the judgment debtor to pay $50.00 per pay period toward payment of the judgment. The levying officer may return any funds now held to the judgment debtor. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)
